SEARCH
Package com.ggalmazor.downsampling

Class PerceptuallyImportantPoints

java.lang.Object
com.ggalmazor.downsampling.PerceptuallyImportantPoints
public final class PerceptuallyImportantPoints extends Object
Perceptually Important Points (PIP) time-series downsampling algorithm.

PIP is an iterative greedy algorithm that builds the output set starting from the first and last points, then repeatedly finds and adds the remaining point that has the largest perpendicular distance from the line connecting its two currently-selected neighbours, until the desired number of output points is reached.

Unlike RDP, PIP always produces exactly targetSize output points (or all input points if the input is smaller).

The implementation uses a max segment tree over the squared perpendicular distances array. This gives O(log n) for both "find the unselected point with the maximum distance" and "update the distance of a point after its neighbours change". Total complexity is O(n + k log n): O(n) to build the initial tree, O(k log n) to select k points each requiring O(segment length) distance updates, each O(log n). There are no stale heap entries, no garbage accumulation, and no dependence on Java's PriorityQueue lacking a decreaseKey operation.

The input list must be sorted by Point.x() in monotonically non-decreasing order. This class does not mutate the input list or its elements.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static <U extends Point>
    List<U>
    select(List<U> input, int targetSize)
    Returns a downsampled version of the provided sorted input list with exactly targetSize points (or all points if targetSize >= input.size()).

  • Method Details

    • select

      public static <U extends Point> List<U> select(List<U> input, int targetSize)
      Returns a downsampled version of the provided sorted input list with exactly targetSize points (or all points if targetSize >= input.size()).
      Type Parameters:
      U - the type of the Point elements in the input list
      Parameters:
      input - the sorted input list of Point points to downsample
      targetSize - the desired total number of output points including the first and last
      Returns:
      the downsampled output list
